applicability: Robust developmental systems with multi-modal feedback.
Less useful for isolated single-cell fate transitions.
definition: A cross-modal developmental constraint preserving a tissue's reachable shape-and-fate trajectory through coupled gene-expression, mechanical, geometric, and signaling states.
failure_modes: Could rename canalization or morphogenetic field.
Needs trajectory reconstruction data.
predictions: Perturbations preserving trajectory constraints will recover form better than single-pathway restoration.
Developmental failures will cluster by broken trajectory constraint rather than individual pathway.

applicability: Any scientific domain.
Too broad without domain-specific constraints.
definition: The underlying layer of variables from which higher-level explanations can be constructed across a domain.
failure_modes: Nearly content-free.
Renames representation or ontology.
predictions: Better substrates should allow more explanations to be derived with fewer assumptions.

applicability: Communication, coding, inference, and statistical signals.
Does not by itself capture meaning or value.
definition: A meaning-independent measure of uncertainty reduction and channel-limited communication structure.
failure_modes: Semantic overextension.
Misuse when message probabilities are undefined.
predictions: Code length and channel capacity depend on statistical uncertainty rather than semantic content.
Noise and redundancy trade off under formal limits.

applicability: All domains by definition.
No clear falsification boundary.
definition: A universal causal property by which all variables influence all other variables at all scales through hidden dependency paths.
failure_modes: Destroys causal discrimination.
Explains everything and predicts little.
predictions: Every system should reveal causal influence if measured at sufficient resolution.

applicability: Interpretable systems with probe and ablation access.
Not a primitive if it only summarizes measurements.
definition: A compact signature of an internal mechanism based on its response profile to probes, ablations, and context changes.
failure_modes: Could be only a metric.
May depend heavily on probe choice.
predictions: Mechanisms with similar fingerprints will show similar transfer behavior.

applicability: Thermal systems with definable macroscopic states.
Less direct in far-from-equilibrium cases without additional formalism.
definition: A state quantity that tracks the directionality and unavailable-work structure of thermodynamic transformations.
failure_modes: Can be confused with disorder metaphors.
Requires careful state-space definition.
predictions: Reversible and irreversible heat processes separate by state-change constraints.
Heat-engine efficiency limits can be expressed without treating heat as a conserved substance.

applicability: Neural representation and compressed distributed coding.
Less useful if representations are already disentangled.
definition: A representational regime in which more features are encoded than there are obvious orthogonal representation dimensions, creating interference and polysemantic units.
failure_modes: Can overexplain unrelated polysemanticity.
Depends on feature definitions.
predictions: Sparse features can share representational dimensions.
Interference patterns will depend on feature frequency and geometry.

applicability: Heritable biological variation.
Weak where inheritance is purely cultural or environmental.
definition: A transmissible hereditary unit separable from visible trait expression and environmental realization.
failure_modes: Can be overtreated as a single fixed physical object.
Gene expression and regulation complicate simple unit language.
predictions: Inheritance patterns can remain stable even when phenotypes vary.
Crosses should reveal latent transmissible factors distinct from appearance.

applicability: Strategic or adaptive evaluated systems.
Less useful for static classifiers or fully observable systems.
definition: Loss of truth-correlation in an evaluation or supervision process as the evaluated system changes its capabilities, incentives, explanations, or interaction strategy.
failure_modes: Could rename Goodharting.
Too broad unless truth-correlation is measured.
predictions: Static evaluations will fail when capability changes the evidence-generation process.
Explicit channel-drift probes will catch some dangerous failures earlier than accuracy-only evaluations.

applicability: Electromagnetism, gravitation, continuum physics.
Less useful where point interactions are sufficient.
definition: A spatially distributed state assigning physically relevant quantities throughout a region rather than only at source bodies.
failure_modes: Can hide mechanism if treated as pure metaphor.
Requires measurable field variables.
predictions: Influence can propagate through local spatial structure.
Patterns between bodies can predict later forces and waves.

applicability: Trained systems with intervention access.
Does not require human-understandable labels.
Weak where roles are context-created rather than retained.
definition: An equivalence class of internal states, features, subspaces, or circuits that preserves the same intervention-stable input-output role across prompts, bases, model instances, and scale.
failure_modes: May reduce to causal abstraction if too broad.
May be unmeasurable in closed models.
May add no value beyond current feature/circuit terms.
predictions: Cross-model mechanisms with high carrier overlap will transfer steering and editing effects better than mechanisms matched only by feature labels.
Some features with different human labels will collapse into the same carrier under intervention tests.
Carrier stability will predict which explanations survive model scaling.

applicability: Coupled systems with measured interaction pathways.
Less useful for isolated tipping elements.
definition: Accumulated cross-system destabilizing burden imposed by interacting tipping elements before any one element crosses its isolated threshold.
failure_modes: Could rename tipping cascade risk.
Interaction estimates may remain too uncertain.
predictions: Network load will predict multi-element tipping risk better than distance to the closest isolated threshold.
Sub-threshold perturbations in one element will reduce resilience in linked elements.

applicability: Formal computation, proof procedures, machine execution.
Less direct for informal heuristics without a precise step rule.
definition: A finite, rule-governed effective procedure whose steps determine a class of computable transformations.
failure_modes: Can be used too loosely for any method.
Resource limits require extra complexity concepts.
predictions: Some tasks admit mechanical procedures while others do not.
Procedure identity can be studied independently of human intuition.

applicability: Systems where interventions can be repeated across settings.
Weak where no stable intervention target exists.
definition: A set of internal model components treated as identical when the same intervention produces the same downstream behavioral change across settings.
failure_modes: Likely duplicates causal-role or causal-abstraction language.
May collapse distinct mechanisms with same output effect.
predictions: Matched mechanisms should transfer ablation and steering effects across contexts.

applicability: Poorly specified self-organizing systems.
Fails where no preferred direction is justified.
definition: A quantity measuring how much a process tends to become more organized when observed from a preferred explanatory direction.
failure_modes: Confuses observer description with physical process.
Likely conflicts with existing entropy unless carefully restricted.
predictions: Systems with high reverse entropy should spontaneously generate simpler explanations.

applicability: Causal modeling, mechanistic explanation, abstraction in AI systems.
Needs explicit intervention semantics.
definition: A relation between lower-level and higher-level causal models where macro variables preserve relevant intervention structure.
failure_modes: Can become vacuous if every mapping counts.
Requires a chosen high-level variable set.
predictions: High-level explanations transfer when interventions commute with the lower-level model.
Some coarse-grainings preserve causal role while others destroy it.
